REGION_BUNDLE.climate: Coastal-to-interior gradient: Prince Rupert is one of Canada's wettest and cloudiest cities (about 2,600 mm of rain a year), while Terrace and the Bulkley Valley inland get real winters with heavy snow; Haida Gwaii is mild, wet and wind-battered.
REGION_BUNDLE.terrain: The Skeena and Bulkley river valleys cutting through the Coast Mountains to the North Coast fjords; Haida Gwaii archipelago offshore; vast wilderness north along the Stewart-Cassiar Highway.
REGION_BUNDLE.economy: The Port of Prince Rupert (North America's closest port to Asia, a fast-growing container terminal), LNG Canada at Kitimat (Canada's largest private-sector construction project), Rio Tinto's aluminium smelter, salmon fishing on the Skeena, and Indigenous-led economies including Haida Gwaii tourism.
REGION_BUNDLE.building_stock: Rain-screened coastal housing in Prince Rupert, company-town stock in Kitimat, valley homesteads around Smithers and Terrace, and remote off-grid properties; marine-grade materials matter on the coast.
